Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Electron beam powder bed fusion of Y2O3/γ-TiAl nanocomposite with balanced strength and toughness

Process_Text.original: For the reference sample, three outlines with 0.5mm offset (red lines in Fig. 3a) were produced during the contour melt whilst the electron beam meandered in a back-and-forth pattern during the hatch melt (black lines in Fig. 3a), with the 90° scan rotation between layers. The pattern was formed by continuous movement of the electron beam (blue point in Fig. 3b). A layer thickness of 70μm was used. Table 1 summarises the process parameters: hatch melt: ν=2400 mm/s, I=8 mA, L_off=0.1 mm, FO=0.2 mA. Build temperature: 1160°C, vacuum: 1×10⁻² mbar controlled by He gas.
Process_Text.simplified: Hatch melt strategy with snake-like scan pattern, 90° rotation between layers. Parameters: scanning speed 2400 mm/s, beam current 8 mA, line offset 0.1 mm, focus offset 0.2 mA. Layer thickness 70 μm. Build temperature 1160°C.

Microstructure_Text.original: The EBSD determined phase constitution in the hatch melt sample is 14.3% α2, 70.4% γ and 15.3% B2. The nanoparticles have a mean size of 218 ± 113 nm and some particles are arranged in a chain-like pattern. Compared to the multi-spot sample, the nanoparticle size in the hatch melt sample is larger and its distribution is less uniform, which is corresponding to the lower nanoparticle number density of 0.09±0.03μm⁻² and similar volume fraction of 0.9±0.4%. The lamellar interface is straight and the lamellar spacing was measured to be 959±155 nm. Dislocations and twins can be found in both the equiaxed γ grains and γ lamellae, but no evidence suggesting their presence in α2 and B2 grains. Dislocations are blocked by twins and grain boundaries. Some dislocations are arranged in a parallel pattern, suggesting the twin formation mechanism via the dislocation pile-up at boundaries. The merging of twins proceeded by movement of small steps along the twin interface, suggesting the 'terrace-ledge-kink' mechanism. In addition, the presence of dislocation sub-grain boundary within γ lamellae indicates that the internal stress during the PBF-EB process is high enough to trigger the recovery and recrystallisation of γ-TiAl.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Duplex microstructure with α2/γ lamellar colonies, equiaxed γ and B2 grains. Phase fractions: α2 14.3%, γ 70.4%, B2 15.3%. Lamellar spacing 959±155 nm. Nanoparticles (Y2O3) size 218±113 nm, number density 0.09±0.03 μm⁻², volume fraction 0.9±0.4%, distributed in chain-like pattern. Dislocations and twins present in γ phase, no twins in α2 or B2.

Process_Text.original: By comparison, the multi-spot melt strategy created multiple closed loops along the perimeter of the sample on the x-y plane (black lines in Fig. 3c). The whole build area was subjected to a dummy scan upon the completion of each loop to ensure the same scanning time despite different loop perimeters. For this purpose, average beam current of I = 25mA, scanning speed of ν = 150000mm/s and line offset of L_off = 2mm were chosen. The enlarged view of Fig. 3c presents a sketch with the numbers indicating the spot sequence. In this case, four concurrent beam spots were utilised. Fig. 3d shows the scanning pattern formed by the first spot of each short sections (blue) for the pre-defined loops. Table 1 summarises the process parameters: multi-spot: ν=600 mm/s, I=25 mA, L_off=0.5 mm, FO=2.5 mA, E_L=2.5 J/mm, n=50, t=0.8 ms, l=4 mm? Layer thickness: 70 μm (L70). Build temperature: 1160°C, vacuum: 1×10⁻² mbar.
Process_Text.simplified: Multi-spot melt strategy with closed loops and dummy scan, four concurrent beam spots. Parameters: scanning speed 600 mm/s, beam current 25 mA, line offset 0.5 mm, focus offset 2.5 mA, line energy 2.5 J/mm, spot exposure time 0.8 ms. Layer thickness 70 μm. Build temperature 1160°C.

Microstructure_Text.original: The multi-spot sample has a phase constitution of 49.7% α2-phase, 40.2% γ-phase and 10.1% B2-phase, as illustrated by the EBSD phase map in Fig. 5a. The matrix exhibits a duplex microstructure consisting of α2/γ lamellar colonies, equiaxed γ and B2 grains, as shown by the BSE-SEM image in Fig. 5b. The inset in Fig. 5b shows the uniform distribution of nanoparticles without agglomeration and slagging. The size, number density and volume fraction of nanoparticles were determined as 90±38nm, 0.28±0.14μm⁻² and 0.8±0.2% respectively. The α2/γ lamellar interface is straight and the lamellar spacing was measured to be 208±72nm. Many twin intersections and dislocations can be seen in the equiaxed γ grains, suggesting that the internal stress generated during the PBF-EB process is likely to be accommodated by deformation of γ grains. HRTEM shows stacking faults at the twin interface, indicating twin formation involves dislocation slip on consecutive (111)γ planes. Twins develop along different directions, and secondary twinning is observed. Twins develop both along the interface and within the γ lamellae. The twin lamellae within γ lamellae can propagate across the twin interface but terminate at the γ/α2 interface.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Duplex microstructure: α2/γ lamellar colonies, equiaxed γ and B2 grains. Phase fractions: α2 49.7%, γ 40.2%, B2 10.1%. Lamellar spacing 208±72 nm. Uniformly distributed Y2O3 nanoparticles: size 90±38 nm, number density 0.28±0.14 μm⁻², volume fraction 0.8±0.2%. Twins and dislocations present in γ grains and γ lamellae; twins propagate across twin interfaces but stop at γ/α2 interface.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Tensile test at 800°C, strain rate 2×10⁻⁴ s⁻¹
Tensile test at 800°C, strain rate 2×10⁻⁴ s⁻¹

Room temperature, single-edge notched bend (SENB) per ASTM E399

Composition_Info.Note: 0.4wt% Y2O3 nanoparticles added; measured composition not reported.

Process_Text.original: Same multi-spot melt strategy as L70 but with layer thickness of 90μm. Process parameters from Table 1: ν=600 mm/s, I=25 mA, L_off=0.5 mm, FO=2.5 mA, E_L=2.5 J/mm, t=0.8 ms. Build temperature: 1160°C, vacuum: 1×10⁻² mbar.
Process_Text.simplified: Multi-spot melt strategy with layer thickness 90 μm. Same beam parameters as L70: scanning speed 600 mm/s, beam current 25 mA, line offset 0.5 mm, focus offset 2.5 mA, line energy 2.5 J/mm, spot exposure time 0.8 ms. Build temperature 1160°C.

Microstructure_Text.original: For L90 (90μm layer thickness), the bulk microstructure exhibits a duplex type with fully lamellar top layer transforming to duplex due to thermal cycling. The α2-phase fraction is 12% (considerably lower than L70's 50%), γ-phase fraction ~75%. The lamellar colony fraction reduces sharply near top layer. Coarsening of γ lamellae and dissolution of α2 proceeds by movement of small steps along α2/γ interface, indicating diffusion-controlled step mechanism. Dislocation slip in γ-phase induces twin formation or blocked at boundaries. Some γ sub-grains are seen at lamellar interfaces and across α2 and γ lamellae, implying recrystallisation drives lamellar degradation. Nanoparticle size and spacing not quantitatively reported.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Duplex microstructure with lower α2 fraction (12%) and higher γ fraction (~75%) compared to L70. Lamellar coarsening and α2 dissolution observed. Nanoparticle data not quantified. More microstructure degradation due to larger layer thickness.
